message:
feat(resolv): add __b64_ntop + __b64_pton (BIND base64) (bd-d8us8)

BIND/libresolv RFC 4648 base64 encoder + decoder. Used by name
servers, DNSSEC tooling, and anything linking against
libresolv.so.2 (where these are the actual exported symbols). The
existing crypt(3) and System V (a64l) base64 modules use a
different alphabet, no padding, and LSB-first bit packing — they
can't be reused.

* core::resolv::b64::ntop / pton — pure-byte RFC 4648 with `=`
  padding, MSB-first bit packing, whitespace tolerance in pton
  (libresolv allows wrapped base64), canonical-encoding enforcement
  (rejects non-zero leftover bits per RFC 4648 §3.5).
* glibc_internal_abi shims expose b64_ntop / b64_pton plus the
  __b64_ntop / __b64_pton aliases that libresolv actually exports.
  All four return -1 on error per BIND convention.

17 core unit tests (RFC §10 vectors, full-byte-range round trip,
canonical-bits enforcement, target-too-small, padding shape) +
12 abi integration tests (NULL guards, wrapped input, alias
equivalence, 200-byte arbitrary round trip). Clippy-clean,
fmt-clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
